title: Uzbekistan-China Economic Cooperation Forum Held Successfully
excerpt: A two-day high-level forum on strengthening economic cooperation between Uzbekistan and China was successfully held in Tashkent.
content:
A two-day high-level forum on strengthening economic cooperation between Uzbekistan and China was successfully held in Tashkent.

The forum discussed issues of developing economic ties, increasing trade volumes, and implementing new investment projects. More than 150 delegates participated.

According to the Association Chairman, this forum enables the bilateral relationship to reach a new level. A total of 15 memorandums and agreements were signed.

A 40% increase in trade volume is planned for the coming year, with significant new investment projects also confirmed.

title: Recommendations for Improving Investment Climate in Uzbekistan Developed
excerpt: Association experts have prepared a comprehensive set of recommendations for further improving the investment climate in Uzbekistan.
content:
Association experts have prepared a comprehensive set of recommendations for further improving the investment climate in Uzbekistan.

The recommendations cover tax policy, reducing bureaucratic barriers, mechanisms for supporting entrepreneurship, and compliance with international standards.

The document was submitted to the relevant government agencies and a discussion process has begun. Experts note that implementing these recommendations could increase foreign investment flows into the country by 30%.

The Association plans to hold a broad public discussion on these recommendations next month.

title: 12 New Member Organizations Join the Association
excerpt: In the first quarter of 2026, 12 major enterprises and organizations joined the Association as new members.
content:
In the first quarter of 2026, 12 major enterprises and organizations joined the Association as new members.

Among the new members are large companies operating in manufacturing, agriculture, finance, and IT. Their combined annual turnover exceeds $500 million.

The Association Chairman congratulated the new members and expressed hope for further development of partnership relations. Currently, the Association has 162 member organizations.

An orientation seminar for new members is planned for next week.

title: Export Development Program Launched
excerpt: The Association announced a new program aimed at expanding the export capabilities of Uzbekistan's enterprises.
content:
The Association announced a new program aimed at expanding the international market opportunities for Uzbekistan's enterprises.

Under the program, local enterprises will be able to receive free consultations on international trade standards, marketing strategies, and export regulations.

Over 100 enterprises are planned to benefit from this program in 2026. Special focus will be given to markets in Germany, France, South Korea, and UAE.

Registration is available through the Association's official website.

title: Association's Annual Session 2026 Held
excerpt: At the next annual session of the Association, the results of 2025 were discussed and the plan for 2026 was approved.
content:
At the next annual session of the Association, the results of 2025 were discussed and the work plan for 2026 was approved.

More than 80 delegates participated in the session. Main discussions were devoted to investment activity, export volumes, and international cooperation.

According to the data, in 2025, the total export volume of Association members increased by 12%. High results were also noted in attracting foreign investments.

The plan for the coming year includes new international forums, signing cooperation agreements, and domestic market development programs.

title: Cooperation with Western Countries Strengthened
excerpt: The Association signed cooperation memorandums with partner organizations from the European Union and the United States.
content:
The Association signed a number of cooperation memorandums with partner organizations from European Union countries and the United States of America.

The signed documents define cooperation in the areas of economic data exchange, joint investment projects, and expert exchanges.

EU representatives highly praised the growth rate of Uzbekistan's economy and expressed their intention to further expand cooperation.

These agreements will further strengthen Uzbekistan's role in international economic cooperation and open new opportunities.
